Barbell Bench Press.description: A compound chest exercise performed lying on a flat bench, pressing a barbell from chest level to full arm extension.
instructions: Lie flat on the bench with feet firmly on the floor
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width
Unrack the bar and hold it above your chest with arms extended
Lower the bar slowly to your mid-chest
Press the bar back up to the starting position
form_cues: Keep shoulder blades retracted and squeezed together
Maintain a slight arch in your lower back
Drive your feet into the floor for stability
Control the descent — don't bounce off your chest
common_mistakes: Flaring elbows too wide (keep ~45-75 degree angle)
Lifting hips off the bench
Not fully locking out at the top
Uneven grip width
Barbell Bench Press.breathing: Inhale on the way down, exhale as you press up

Barbell Back Squat.description: The king of lower body exercises. A compound movement targeting quads, glutes, and core with a barbell across the upper back.
instructions: Position the bar on your upper traps (high bar) or rear delts (low bar)
Step back and set feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
Brace your core and take a deep breath
Break at the hips and knees simultaneously
Descend until thighs are at least parallel to the floor
Drive through your whole foot to stand back up
form_cues: Keep your chest up and eyes forward
Push knees out over toes — don't let them cave in
Keep the bar path over midfoot
Maintain a neutral spine throughout
common_mistakes: Knees caving inward (valgus collapse)
Rounding the lower back at the bottom
Rising on toes / heels coming off the floor
Not hitting depth (aim for parallel or below)
Barbell Back Squat.breathing: Big breath in and brace before descent, exhale past the sticking point

Conventional Deadlift.description: A full-body compound lift pulling a barbell from the floor to hip height. Builds total body strength.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, bar over midfoot
Hinge at hips, grip the bar just outside your knees
Drop hips, lift chest, and pull slack out of the bar
Drive through the floor, keeping the bar close to your body
Lock out by squeezing glutes at the top
Reverse the movement to lower the bar
form_cues: Keep the bar close to your shins and thighs
Think of pushing the floor away, not pulling the bar up
Engage your lats — protect your lower back
Lock out with your hips, not by leaning back
common_mistakes: Rounding the lower back
Bar drifting away from the body
Jerking the bar off the floor (pull the slack out first)
Hyperextending at the top
Conventional Deadlift.breathing: Big breath and brace before the pull, exhale at lockout

Overhead Press.description: A standing compound shoulder exercise pressing a barbell from shoulder level overhead.
instructions: Start with the bar resting on your front delts, hands just outside shoulders
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes
Press the bar straight up, moving your head back slightly to clear the bar path
Once the bar passes your forehead, push your head through
Lock out with the bar directly over midfoot
form_cues: Keep your ribs down — don't flare them
Squeeze glutes hard to prevent lower back arch
The bar should travel in a straight vertical line
Full lockout at the top — biceps by ears
common_mistakes: Excessive lower back arch (leaning back too far)
Pressing the bar forward instead of straight up
Not locking out fully overhead
Using leg drive (that makes it a push press)
Overhead Press.breathing: Breath in at the bottom, brace, exhale as you press

Pull-Up.description: A bodyweight compound back exercise hanging from a bar and pulling yourself up until chin clears the bar.
instructions: Hang from the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder width, palms facing away
Retract your shoulder blades and engage your lats
Pull yourself up until your chin is above the bar
Lower yourself under control back to a full hang
form_cues: Initiate the pull by driving your elbows down and back
Keep your core tight — no swinging
Full range of motion: dead hang to chin over bar
Control the descent — don't just drop
common_mistakes: Using momentum / kipping
Not going to full extension at the bottom
Chin not clearing the bar
Shrugging shoulders up instead of pulling with lats
Pull-Up.breathing: Exhale as you pull up, inhale on the way down

Romanian Deadlift.description: A hip hinge movement targeting the hamstrings and glutes, performed with a slight knee bend.
instructions: Hold the bar at hip height with an overhand grip
With a slight bend in your knees, push your hips back
Lower the bar along your thighs and shins
Go as low as your hamstring flexibility allows
Drive hips forward to return to standing
form_cues: Keep the bar close to your body at all times
Feel the stretch in your hamstrings
Maintain a neutral spine — no rounding
Knees stay in the same slightly bent position throughout
common_mistakes: Bending the knees too much (this becomes a conventional deadlift)
Rounding the back
Letting the bar drift away from the body
Not going deep enough to feel the hamstring stretch
Romanian Deadlift.breathing: Inhale on the way down, exhale driving hips forward

Hip Thrust.description: The primary glute builder. Upper back on a bench, driving hips up with a barbell across the hips.
instructions: Sit on the floor with upper back against a bench, barbell over hips
Plant feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart
Drive through your heels to lift hips until fully extended
Squeeze glutes hard at the top
Lower hips back down under control
form_cues: Tuck your chin slightly — look forward, not up
Drive through your heels, not your toes
Full hip extension at the top — squeeze for a count
Feet positioned so shins are vertical at the top
common_mistakes: Hyperextending the lower back instead of squeezing glutes
Feet too far forward or too close
Not reaching full hip extension
Rushing the movement
Hip Thrust.breathing: Exhale and squeeze at the top, inhale on the way down

Lateral Raise.description: An isolation exercise for the side deltoids, performed by raising dumbbells out to the sides.
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at your sides, slight bend in elbows
Raise both arms out to the sides until parallel with the floor
Pause briefly at the top
Lower slowly back to the starting position
form_cues: Lead with your elbows, not your hands
Slight forward lean helps target the side delt
Think of pouring water out of a pitcher at the top
Don't go heavier than you can control
common_mistakes: Using too much weight and swinging
Raising above shoulder height (engages traps too much)
Straight arms (keep a slight elbow bend)
Leading with the hands instead of elbows
Lateral Raise.breathing: Exhale as you raise, inhale as you lower

Barbell Curl.description: A classic bicep isolation exercise curling a barbell from hip level to shoulder height.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, grip the bar underhand at shoulder width
Keep your upper arms stationary at your sides
Curl the bar up by bending your elbows
Squeeze at the top, then lower under control
form_cues: Keep your elbows pinned to your sides
Don't swing your body — strict form
Control the negative (lowering phase)
Full range of motion: full extension to full contraction
common_mistakes: Swinging the body for momentum
Elbows drifting forward
Not going to full extension at the bottom
Using too much weight at the expense of form
Barbell Curl.breathing: Exhale on the curl up, inhale on the way down

Plank.description: An isometric core exercise holding a push-up position on forearms, building core stability and endurance.
instructions: Place forearms on the floor, elbows under shoulders
Extend legs back, toes on the floor
Lift your body to form a straight line from head to heels
Hold the position, engaging your entire core
form_cues: Don't let your hips sag or pike up
Squeeze your glutes and quads
Keep your neck neutral — look at the floor
Breathe steadily — don't hold your breath
common_mistakes: Hips sagging toward the floor
Hips too high (piking up)
Holding breath
Looking forward instead of down
Plank.breathing: Breathe normally throughout — steady in and out
